title: Error instability in V3SplitVar
body:
@yTakatsukasa

1. We noticed that the t_split_var_1_bad test was occasionally failing e.g.

https://travis-ci.com/github/verilator/verilator/jobs/343524573

The test fails because the error output changes.  I believe this is because in V3SplitVar.cpp's split() there is an unordered_map which can report errors.  

Would you be willing to take a look?   Note we wouldn't want to just make it a std::map as a key of pointers still would not be stable - it needs some stable sort.

2.  I've been trying to look at code coverage, if you look at the coverage report here: https://codecov.io/gh/verilator/verilator/src/master/src/V3SplitVar.cpp

you'll see a bunch of lines that the tests aren't covering. My primary concern would be lines unrelated to errors, but ideally we'd cover the user errors too.  No need to cover internal errors - I tried to turn off coverage on all internal-only errors but might have missed some.

Thanks!
